title: verbascum macrocarpum
intro: Verbascum macrocarpum, also known as great mullein, is a biennial plant species in the figwort family that is native to southern Europe and western Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of Verbascum macrocarum include its ability to soothe irritated mucous membranes, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. In herbal preparations, Verbascum macrocarpum is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be used topically in ointments and salves to treat skin irritations and wounds.
